message:
ffs-fuse: fix FS_IOC_GET_ENCRYPTION_POLICY_EX constant + refactor ioctl plumbing

Several related ffs-fuse / fuse-e2e / fuzz changes that all land
together because they share the `FS_IOC_GET_ENCRYPTION_POLICY_EX`
constant value and because the fuse crate's ioctl / tracing /
move_ext plumbing shares call sites with them.

### The ioctl constant fix

`FS_IOC_GET_ENCRYPTION_POLICY_EX` was encoded as `0xC016_6616`
across three files:

- `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs` (const)
- `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` (Rust const + embedded
  Python script literal)
- `fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_ioctl_dispatch.rs` (const)

The `0x16` (decimal 22) in the size field came from an early
understanding of the ioctl as `_IOWR('f', 22, struct fscrypt_get
_policy_ex_arg)` — size of the arg struct. Linux's actual kernel
definition is `#define FS_IOC_GET_ENCRYPTION_POLICY_EX
_IOWR('f', 22, __u8[9])` — the data-length field in the ioctl
number is just 9 (the header size, not the whole arg). Against a
real kernel, the ioctl dispatcher compares the full ioctl number
including the size field, so our value was silently failing for
any caller that matched against `/usr/include/linux/fs.h` or the
`FS_IOC_GET_ENCRYPTION_POLICY_EX` macro produced by fscrypt
userspace.

Flip all three constants to `0xC009_6616`. No behavioural change
for code that matches against our own constant; now
cross-platform-compatible with userspace fscrypt tools.

The `fuse_e2e.rs` embedded Python probe also had the literal
`0xC0166616` hex value in a multi-line `r"..."` script. Updated
in lockstep. The script still runs under the same Python
interpreter invoked by `python3 -c` with `Stdio` piped in, so no
other plumbing changed.

### IoctlTraceProbe: don't panic when the writer thread can't spawn

`IoctlTraceProbe::new(path)` previously built the background
writer with `thread::Builder::new().name(...).spawn(...).expect(
"spawn ioctl trace writer thread")`. Under resource exhaustion
(rlimit, cgroup max_threads, TSAN harness limits) that panic
reached the fuse driver's startup path and aborted the whole
process even though ioctl tracing is purely diagnostic.

Replace the expect with a match:
- Ok(worker) — stash the JoinHandle and the sender.
- Err(error) — emit a warn! log (`path = %path.display(), %error,
  "disabling ioctl trace because writer thread could not be
  spawned"`) and proceed with `sender = None`, `worker = None`.

Downstream send sites already guard on `Option<sender>` via the
existing probe shape, so a probe that couldn't spawn its worker
is a no-op tracer rather than a crash.

### MoveExtLogContext → MoveExtLogRecord refactor

The MOVE_EXT ioctl audit-log helper was building its log record
inline from 11+ loose locals (`scenario_id`, `outcome`,
`error_class`, `ino`, `donor_ino`, ...). Extract them into a new
`struct MoveExtLogRecord<'a> { ... }` that lives adjacent to the
pre-existing `MoveExtLogContext`. The record is the *output*
shape (what we emit to the structured log), while the context is
the *input* shape (what the caller still owns). Logging sites now
build a `MoveExtLogRecord` and hand it to the emitter; future
schema additions can grow the record struct in one place.

### `setattr_for_fuzzing` takes `&SetAttrRequest`

The fuzz helper `FrankenFuse::setattr_for_fuzzing(ino, attrs)`
took `SetAttrRequest` by value. Every fuzz-harness caller had to
clone a fresh request for every mutation. Flip to
`&SetAttrRequest` so the harness can reuse a single prototype and
cycle through per-field mutations without the clone. No ABI
impact — this is an in-tree fuzz helper only. Downstream fuzz
targets already existed that expected the reference form; this
change aligns the definition.

### fuzz_ioctl_dispatch: seed the policy_ex header size field

`get_encryption_policy_ex_request(cursor)` was returning the raw
fuzzer bytes as the ioctl arg, padded to `FSCRYPT_POLICY_EX_HEADER_SIZE
+ FSCRYPT_POLICY_V[12]_SIZE`. Without a matching header
`policy_size` field, every request was rejected by the dispatcher
before exercising any of the policy-version dispatching logic,
meaning 100% of the harness's coverage budget for this ioctl was
spent on the pre-dispatch size-mismatch branch.

Rewrite: if the constructed request is at least as long as the
header, write `policy_size = request.len() -
FSCRYPT_POLICY_EX_HEADER_SIZE` (as `u64` in native-endian) into
the leading 8 bytes. The fuzzer-supplied payload bytes that lived
there previously are overwritten by a well-formed length so the
dispatcher accepts the request and the downstream path gets
exercised. Side effect: the harness now explores the real
V1/V2/size-mismatch branches from inside the dispatcher instead
of bouncing off the front door.

### fuse_e2e symlink target length + process stdio

Small adjacent touch-ups in the same file:

- Extract `const MAX_SYMLINK_TARGET_LEN: usize = LINUX_PATH_MAX -
  1` next to the existing `LINUX_PATH_MAX = 4096` constant, so the
  boundary tests that assert on exactly 4095-byte targets cite a
  named constant instead of the inline `4095` or `4096 - 1` that
  was scattered across three asserts. No value change.
- Add `use std::process::Stdio;` and route the embedded-Python
  ioctl probe through explicit stdio handles rather than letting
  Command inherit the parent's fds. That keeps test-wide stdout
  clean when these probes run during CI log collection.

No public API change in any of the three crates; this is
constant-value + refactor + fuzz-harness plumbing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
